info.name: Event Hub API
info.description: REST + GraphQL endpoints for the Event Hub backend (.NET 8 / EF Core / SQL Server). Uses {{baseUrl}} — set this in the environment (e.g. https://localhost:5001).

request.description: Returns 201 with the created VenueDto. Save the returned id into {{venueId}} for subsequent requests.
200 with VenueDto, or 404 if not found / soft-deleted.
200 with a flat array of VenueDto. No pagination on REST (cursor pagination is a GraphQL-only concern for Events).
200 with updated VenueDto, or 404 if not found. Venue has no RowVersion — no concurrency conflict possible here.
204 on success. Sets IsDeleted = true; row is retained. Subsequent GET/List calls will no longer show it due to the global query filter.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Annual Tech Meetup",
  "description": "A gathering for local developers.",
  "startDate": "2026-09-15T18:00:00Z",
  "capacity": 2,
  "venueId": "{{venueId}}"
}

request.description: 201 with EventDto (includes RowVersion as base64). 422 if venueId doesn't exist. New events are created in Draft status. Save the returned id into {{eventId}} and rowVersion into {{rowVersion}}.
200 with EventDto, or 404 if not found / soft-deleted.
200 with a flat array of EventDto. For paginated/nested (with Venue) queries, use the GraphQL endpoint instead.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Annual Tech Meetup",
  "description": "A gathering for local developers.",
  "startDate": "2026-09-15T18:00:00Z",
  "capacity": 2,
  "status": "Published",
  "rowVersion": "{{rowVersion}}"
}

request.description:
200 with updated EventDto (and a fresh RowVersion — update {{rowVersion}} after each successful call), or:
- 409 if RowVersion is stale (someone else updated it first)
- 422 if the status transition is illegal (e.g. Draft → Completed)
- 400 if RowVersion is malformed

The only Booking endpoint — no GET/list.

Generate a fresh GUID for idempotencyKey per NEW booking attempt (e.g. via Postman's {{$guid}} dynamic variable). Re-sending the SAME idempotencyKey returns the original booking again (still 201) rather than creating a duplicate.

Possible responses:
- 201: booking created (or the original, if idempotencyKey was reused)
- 422: eventId or attendeeId doesn't exist
- 409: event is full, OR event isn't open for booking (Draft/Cancelled), OR this attendee already has a non-cancelled booking for this event

Note: attendeeId must be seeded directly via the database — there's no Attendee REST endpoint in this project.

Cursor pagination via 'first'/'after' args (HotChocolate [UsePaging]). Nested venue field is resolved via a batching DataLoader — confirm in server logs it's a single SQL query per page, not one-per-event.
Reuses the same IEventService.GetByIdAsync as REST. A nonexistent id surfaces the same NotFound message via GraphQL's errors array (not an exception/crash).
Take the endCursor from the previous query's pageInfo and paste it here to fetch the next page.
